Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Write up of my homebrew CPU build 203 upvotes, 39 comments
  2. Using calculus to do number theory 69 upvotes, 11 comments
  3. Show HN: Sub-millisecond VM sandboxes using CoW memory forking 271 upvotes, 63 comments
  4. Nightingale – open-source karaoke app that works with any song on your computer 393 upvotes, 108 comments
  5. OpenRocket 104 upvotes, 24 comments
  6. 2025 Turing award given for quantum information science 34 upvotes, 5 comments
  7. Snowflake AI Escapes Sandbox and Executes Malware 163 upvotes, 44 comments
  8. Celebrating Tony Hoare's mark on computer science 102 upvotes, 28 comments
  9. The pleasures of poor product design 224 upvotes, 78 comments
  10. A Fuzzer for the Toy Optimizer 13 upvotes, 1 comments
  11. Show HN: Hacker News archive (47M+ items, 11.6GB) as Parquet, updated every 5m 95 upvotes, 31 comments
  12. Federal Cyber Experts Called Microsoft's Cloud "A Pile of Shit", yet Approved It 315 upvotes, 134 comments
  13. Wander – A tiny, decentralised tool (just 2 files) to explore the small web 44 upvotes, 15 comments
  14. Rob Pike's Rules of Programming (1989) 645 upvotes, 346 comments
  15. A ngrok-style secure tunnel server written in Rust and Open Source 45 upvotes, 16 comments
  16. Google Engineers Launch "Sashiko" for Agentic AI Code Review of the Linux Kernel 50 upvotes, 19 comments
  17. Death to Scroll Fade 255 upvotes, 140 comments
  18. Machine Payments Protocol (MPP) 88 upvotes, 43 comments
  19. CVE-2026-3888: Important Snap Flaw Enables Local Privilege Escalation to Root 22 upvotes, 7 comments
  20. Restoring the first recording of computer music (2018) 21 upvotes, 8 comments
  21. Oil nears $110 a barrel after gas field strike 66 upvotes, 40 comments
  22. Nvidia NemoClaw 111 upvotes, 75 comments
  23. A dither generator for triangular and hexagonal pixels (2025) 4 upvotes, 0 comments
  24. Show HN: Tmux-IDE, OSS agent-first terminal IDE 10 upvotes, 3 comments
  25. Show HN: Will my flight have Starlink? 64 upvotes, 48 comments
  26. Spotify playing ads for paid subscribers 18 upvotes, 9 comments
  27. AI coding is gambling 130 upvotes, 126 comments
  28. North Korean's 100k fake IT workers net $500M a year for Kim 89 upvotes, 87 comments
  29. Wanter – A tiny, decentralised tool to explore the small web 15 upvotes, 18 comments
  30. Ndea (YC W26) is hiring a symbolic RL search guidance lead 0 upvotes, 0 comments

Last generated 3:00am, 19 March 2026. Source here.
